talk_title: Development and impact of prophylactic HPV virus‑like particle vaccines

biomarkers.name: HPV16 DNA persistence
biomarkers.finding: 41 cases in placebo, 0 cases in vaccine arm (p<0.001)

biomarkers.name: Neutralizing antibody titer
biomarkers.finding: >98% of subjects seroconverted after a single dose; titers remained within 2.5% change per year over 16 years

biomarkers.name: HPV‑related cancer incidence
biomarkers.finding: ≈90% reduction in cervical precancer in vaccinated cohorts; 1.4 million future deaths averted globally

models.name: rabbit papillomavirus model
models.genotype_or_key_features: HPV L1 VLP immunization, challenge with rabbit PV

models.name: bovine papillomavirus model
models.genotype_or_key_features: HPV L1 VLP immunization, challenge with bovine PV

models.name: ESCUDO trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Single‑dose vs two‑dose Gardasil 9 or Cervarix in Costa Rica, n≈2,000

MOA_summary: HPV L1 virus‑like particles self‑assemble into 360‑subunit VLPs that present repetitive neutralizing epitopes, inducing durable, high‑titer antibodies that persist for >15 years and provide sterilizing immunity by blocking the slow, basement‑membrane‑mediated entry of HPV into wounded epithelium.
key_findings: A monovalent HPV16 VLP trial showed 0 persistent infections in the vaccine arm versus 41 in placebo, demonstrating sterilizing immunity.
Single‑dose HPV vaccination achieved non‑inferior efficacy (97%–98%) compared with two doses in the ESCUDO trial, supporting dose de‑escalation.
Antibody titers after a single dose remain flat over 16 years, declining <2.5% per year, indicating long‑lived plasma cell maintenance.
Real‑world data from Denmark show ~90% protection against cervical cancer precursors in women vaccinated ≤16 y.
Global vaccine uptake has risen from 72% to 78% (≥1 dose) and 75% to 80% (up‑to‑date) among US adolescents, contributing to an estimated 1.4 million future deaths averted.
open_questions: What is the minimal antibody titer required for long‑term protection across diverse HPV genotypes?
Can single‑dose schedules be safely extended to older age groups or immunocompromised populations?

patent_or_ip_hints: NIH patents on HPV VLP technology

talk_title: Therapeutic applications of HPV VLPs and VLP‑IR700 conjugates

targets.dependency_context: EMT‑associated HSPG modifications on cancer cells

biomarkers.name: HSPG modification pattern
biomarkers.finding: Strong correlation between EMT/TGF‑β signature and VLP binding across 129 cell lines

biomarkers.name: Tumor response (time to referral)
biomarkers.assay: clinical observation in ocular melanoma trial
biomarkers.finding: Therapeutic dose increased median time to standard‑of‑care referral vs sub‑therapeutic dose (exact values not disclosed)

models.name: Panel of 129 cancer cell lines
models.genotype_or_key_features: Diverse tissue origins; EMT status assessed

models.name: Phase I/II ocular melanoma VLP‑IR700 trial
Phase I/II bladder cancer VLP‑IR700 intravesical trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Local administration via cystoscope, window‑of‑opportunity resection

clinical_data.metric: Time to referral to standard of care (ocular melanoma)
clinical_data.value: significantly longer with therapeutic dose vs sub‑therapeutic dose

clinical_data.metric: Tumor growth arrest (ocular melanoma therapeutic dose)
clinical_data.value: tumors stopped growing during treatment period

clinical_data.metric: Complete pathological response (bladder cancer window trial)
clinical_data.value: 0 residual tumor in resected specimens after VLP‑IR700 treatment

MOA_summary: HPV VLPs bind preferentially to cancer cells displaying EMT‑associated, modified heparan‑sulfate proteoglycans; conjugation with the infrared‑activatable dye IR700 enables light‑triggered membrane disruption and necrotic cell death, while simultaneously releasing neoantigens that prime secondary T‑cell responses, creating a dual‑mode therapeutic platform.
key_findings: HPV VLPs bind >90% of a 129‑cell‑line panel, with binding strongly correlated to EMT/TGF‑β transcriptional programs.
In a Phase I/II ocular melanoma trial, the therapeutic VLP‑IR700 regimen markedly prolonged time to standard‑of‑care referral compared with sub‑therapeutic dosing.
Therapeutic dosing halted tumor growth in ocular melanoma patients, suggesting disease‑modifying activity.
A bladder cancer window‑of‑opportunity study showed complete pathological response after intravesical VLP‑IR700 plus light, indicating potent local tumor ablation.
The VLP‑IR700 platform leverages dual specificity (tumor binding + light activation) to achieve on‑target cytotoxicity with minimal off‑target effects.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Binding is absent on hematopoietic cells lacking the specific HSPG modifications, providing intrinsic tumor selectivity.

combination_strategies.rationale: VLP‑IR700 can delay progression, allowing eye‑preserving management
combination_strategies.phenotype: Extended time to referral and tumor stabilization

combination_strategies.rationale: Localized activation of IR700 after VLP binding in bladder
combination_strategies.phenotype: Complete tumor eradication in window trial

open_questions: What is the optimal light dose and delivery method for deep‑seated tumors?
Can the VLP‑IR700 platform be combined with checkpoint inhibition to amplify secondary T‑cell responses?

patent_or_ip_hints: NIH patents on HPV VLP technology; IP on VLP‑IR700 conjugate with Ora Biosciences
